claim.content: When comparing the measured reduced susceptibility data in the high-density liquid to fixed-node diffusion QMC predictions for a homogeneous two-dimensional electron gas parameterized by conduction-band g-factor g, effective mass m^*, and dielectric constant ε, the proposition is that the reported quantitative agreement between experiment and QMC is robust against reasonable variations of these input parameters: moderate adjustments of ε within experimentally plausible bounds or modest changes in the assumed m^* around 0.7 m_0 do not eliminate the quantitative agreement between the measured χ/χ_0 and the QMC curve, supporting the conclusion that the high-density state behaves as a clean, strongly interacting Fermi liquid rather than being an artifact of over-fitting poorly constrained parameters.
